speaker: JULIET
text: Ay, those attires are best. But, gentle nurse,

speaker: JULIET
text: I pray thee leave me to myself tonight,

speaker: JULIET
text: For I have need of many orisons

speaker: JULIET
text: To move the heavens to smile upon my state,

speaker: JULIET
text: Which, well thou knowest, is cross and full of sin.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: What, are you busy, ho? Need you my help?

speaker: JULIET
text: No, madam, we have culled such necessaries

speaker: JULIET
text: As are behooveful for our state tomorrow.

speaker: JULIET
text: So please you, let me now be left alone,

speaker: JULIET
text: And let the Nurse this night sit up with you,

speaker: JULIET
text: For I am sure you have your hands full all

speaker: JULIET
text: In this so sudden business.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Good night.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Get thee to bed and rest, for thou hast need.

speaker: JULIET
text: Farewell.—God knows when we shall meet again.

speaker: JULIET
text: I have a faint cold fear thrills through my veins

speaker: JULIET
text: That almost freezes up the heat of life.

speaker: JULIET
text: I’ll call them back again to comfort me.—

speaker: JULIET
text: Nurse!—What should she do here?

speaker: JULIET
text: My dismal scene I needs must act alone.

speaker: JULIET
text: Come, vial.

speaker: JULIET
text: What if this mixture do not work at all?

speaker: JULIET
text: Shall I be married then tomorrow morning?

speaker: JULIET
text: No, no, this shall forbid it. Lie thou there.

speaker: JULIET
text: What if it be a poison which the Friar

speaker: JULIET
text: Subtly hath ministered to have me dead,

speaker: JULIET
text: Lest in this marriage he should be dishonored

speaker: JULIET
text: Because he married me before to Romeo?

speaker: JULIET
text: I fear it is. And yet methinks it should not,

speaker: JULIET
text: For he hath still been tried a holy man.

speaker: JULIET
text: How if, when I am laid into the tomb,

speaker: JULIET
text: I wake before the time that Romeo

speaker: JULIET
text: Come to redeem me? There’s a fearful point.

speaker: JULIET
text: Shall I not then be stifled in the vault,

speaker: JULIET
text: To whose foul mouth no healthsome air breathes in,

speaker: JULIET
text: And there die strangled ere my Romeo comes?

speaker: JULIET
text: Or, if I live, is it not very like

speaker: JULIET
text: The horrible conceit of death and night,

speaker: JULIET
text: Together with the terror of the place—

speaker: JULIET
text: As in a vault, an ancient receptacle

speaker: JULIET
text: Where for this many hundred years the bones

speaker: JULIET
text: Of all my buried ancestors are packed;

speaker: JULIET
text: Where bloody Tybalt, yet but green in earth,

speaker: JULIET
text: Lies fest’ring in his shroud; where, as they say,

speaker: JULIET
text: At some hours in the night spirits resort—

speaker: JULIET
text: Alack, alack, is it not like that I,

speaker: JULIET
text: So early waking, what with loathsome smells,

speaker: JULIET
text: And shrieks like mandrakes torn out of the earth,

speaker: JULIET
text: That living mortals, hearing them, run mad—

speaker: JULIET
text: O, if I wake, shall I not be distraught,

speaker: JULIET
text: Environèd with all these hideous fears,

speaker: JULIET
text: And madly play with my forefathers’ joints,

speaker: JULIET
text: And pluck the mangled Tybalt from his shroud,

speaker: JULIET
text: And, in this rage, with some great kinsman’s bone,

speaker: JULIET
text: As with a club, dash out my desp’rate brains?

speaker: JULIET
text: O look, methinks I see my cousin’s ghost

speaker: JULIET
text: Seeking out Romeo that did spit his body

speaker: JULIET
text: Upon a rapier’s point! Stay, Tybalt, stay!

speaker: JULIET
text: Romeo, Romeo, Romeo! Here’s drink. I drink to thee.
